name: SLB Limited

profile.description: SLB (formerly Schlumberger) is the world's largest oilfield services company with 109,000 employees across 100+ countries. Revenue grew 2.7% YoY. Leading digital oilfield transformation.

innovation.label: DEVELOPING
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Revenue grew modestly at 2.7%. AI Deployment (1/2): Digital oilfield solutions with AI-enhanced drilling optimization. Market Share (2/2): World's largest oilfield services company. Product Pipeline (0/2): Incremental improvements. R&D Intensity (1/1): Industry-leading technology investment.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): 'Driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' connects to energy transition. Community Impact (1/2): Global community programs across 100+ countries. Employee Retention (1/2): 109,000 employees with relatively stable workforce. Insider Buying (0/2): No data.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Forward P/E of 17.33, D/E of 42.52%, and dividend yield of 2.03% with 40 years payment history [1]. ROE of 14.07% near target. Margin of 9.26% moderate for oilfield services.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): Physical oilfield operations are immune to AI substitution. AI enhances drilling efficiency and reservoir management. AI Opportunity (0/1): No quantified AI-specific revenue.

infinite_game.summary: SLB's rebranding from Schlumberger and 'balanced planet' messaging suggest genuine long-term energy transition thinking, though core business remains fossil fuel services.

investment_thesis: SLB is the world's largest oilfield services company trading at 17.33x forward earnings with 2.03% dividend yield and 40-year payment history [1]. Digital oilfield leadership provides competitive advantage.
thesis_continued: Revenue growth of only 2.7% reflects maturing oilfield cycle [1]. Margin of 9.26% and ROE of 14.07% are solid but not expanding. Energy transition creates long-term demand uncertainty.
key_catalysts: International activity growth in Middle East and offshore markets
Digital oilfield technology adoption improving service margins
Energy transition services expanding into carbon capture and geothermal
key_risks: Oil price decline reducing global E&P spending
Energy transition accelerating faster than expected, reducing fossil fuel service demand
Competition from Halliburton and Baker Hughes on pricing
sources: Yahoo Finance — SLB Key Statistics, accessed June 2026
